page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r6a.large is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs on 2 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The underlying processor is an AMD EPYC 7H12 running at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz, equipped with 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the instance delivers modest processing and memory throughput. In stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, performance falls within the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only small block speeds of 99.72 GB/sec and large block write speeds of 14.89 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression is measured at 23.59 GB/sec, while single-threaded bzip3 decompression achieves an average tier result of 8.92 GB/sec. Passmark CPU and database operations scores also rank in the bottom 10%, reflecting its positioning as a resource-dense rather than compute-intensive instance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.r6a.large offers a high memory allocation relative to its low vCPU count, making it a viable option for workloads that require a larger memory footprint without demanding high-performance compute cycles. Due to its lower CPU benchmark scores and lack of local storage, it is best suited for lightweight memory-bound applications, caching layers, small database operations, and basic web serving where memory capacity is prioritized over raw processing speed.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r6a.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, running on an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor at 2.6 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture and KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core but lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps. Benchmark results place its CPU and memory read performance mostly in the bottom 10%, though single-threaded bzip3 decompression achieves average tier results. Qualitatively, this instance offers a cost-effective profile for memory-dense, low-compute workloads. It is best suited for lightweight caching, small database tasks, and memory-bound applications that do not require intensive processing power.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for lightweight, memory-bound applications requiring dedicated AMD EPYC resources.

16.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Best suited for lightweight caching and memory-bound workloads
